Leihōkū — “garland of stars” in Hawaiian — is the newest class of premium travel. Offered only on Hawaiian Airlines Boeing 787 aircraft, it is a transformative way to fly with enclosed suites, fully lie-flat seats and luxurious details. Your unique story begins in the sky with Leihōkū. Learn more at the link in our bio.

#Leihoku #HawaiianAirlines #Airplane #787 #AVgeek

February is Mahina ʻŌlelo Hawaiʻi (Hawaiian Language Month)! This past weekend, we celebrated with two Ōlelo Hawai‘i flights from Maui (OGG) and Las Vegas (LAS). During these flights, Hawaiian language-speaking employees greeted and served guests from the boarding gates and throughout their flights. Mahalo to our employees for their dedication to sharing the beautiful Hawaiian language with the world.💜 #PualaniProud

 #HawaiianAirlines #OleloHawaii #HawaiianLanguageMonth

Welcome home! ✈️🌺 This morning, over 1,500 employees and their guests celebrated our first @boeing 787 Dreamliner in Honolulu.

Hawaiian Airlines’ and Boeing's leadership joined employees and local dignitaries at the Charles I. Elliott Maintenance and Cargo Facility to induct N781HA with a special welcome and blessing. The festivities were opened with an ‘oli (chant) by students from Windward Oʻahu’s Mālama Honua Public Charter School and included live music from Hawaiian Slack-Key Artist and Nā Hōkū Hanohano Award-Winner Kawika Kahiapo. 

#HawaiianAirlines #Boeing #Aviation #Airplane #787Dreamliner #787 #AvGeek

Earlier this week we welcomed World War II veteran, Ira "Ike" Schab back to Hawaii. Ike is a 104-year-old Pearl Harbor survivor and served as a U.S. Navy musician aboard the USS Dobbin on the morning of the attack that catapulted the country into World War II.

Mahalo to those who have served and continue to serve our community.

#pearlharbor #hawaii #hawaiianairlines

On June 11th, we gather to commemorate the remarkable legacy of King Kamehameha The Great who, in 1810, united the beautiful Hawaiian Islands under one kingdom, the Kingdom of Hawai‘i. It’s a day when our community comes together to honor our late Ali‘i nui, adorning him with lei and celebration.💜 #KingKamehamehaDay

✨SWEEPSTAKES✨ Celebrate #Pride and join us in our 🌈Rainbow Runway Challenge🌈. Show off your own runway walk and enter for a chance to win 160,000 HawaiianMiles, a 5-night stay at the #TheRoyalHawaiian and an opportunity to dance with Mark at the Honolulu Pride parade in October. Here’s how you can enter: 1️⃣ Follow us at HawaiianAir. 2️⃣ Add your own flair and post your runway walk, inspired by Hawai‘i-born celebrity dancer @Mark Kanemura 3️⃣ Post your video and tag us, #RainbowRunwayChallenge and #Sweepstakes in your caption. 🏳️‍🌈#PrideMonth #AlohaIsAloha NO PURCHASE NECESSARY. 18+/age of maj in 50 U.S./D.C. Ends 6/30. See Rules: HawaiianAirlines.com/OfficialRules.

In honor of Mahina ʻŌlelo Hawaiʻi (Hawaiian Language Month)! We held a Hawaiian language flight on HA90 with service from Honolulu (HNL) - Boston (BOS).✈️ 

Our flight attendants made announcements in ʻŌlelo Hawaiʻi and passed out engagement cards to our passengers, providing talking points and translations so they could communicate with our flight attendants in ʻŌlelo Hawaiʻi.

text:
A special Lei Day surprise for 2,500 of our Hawai‘i airport employees and guests — fresh lei first thing in the morning — which means a lot of hugs, smiles and memories were made, too. We flew from our home base in Honolulu to Kona and continued the #LeiDay surprise.
 
This year, we’re extending the fun to 31 Days of Lei, a monthlong celebration of aloha, so get ready for more fun moments in May. #31DaysofLei @Mastercard

Like mother, like daughter 🌺✈️

Yesterday, we celebrated a milestone in our 93-year legacy: our first mother-daughter pilot crew!  #pualaniproud 

Captain Kamelia Zarka and her daughter, First Officer Maria Zarka, flew the Pacific skies together on a Boeing 717 aircraft, operating a series of Neighbor Island flights.

Captain Kamelia joined Hawaiian Airlines as a flight attendant in 1992 before becoming a pilot in 1999, which led her to be the first Tongan woman to captain a commercial airline. First Officer Maria, who flew with Republic Airlines in New Jersey for two years, was hired by Hawaiian Airlines in April 2022.

text:
This weekend we celebrated Girls in Aviation Day! ✈️👩‍✈️

Our Wahine in Aviation employee resource group worked alongside the Women in Aviation International Aloha Chapter to provide young women between ages 12-17 a peek into what a career in aviation would look like. ✨

Mahalo to our Women in Aviation International Aloha Chapter and our Hawaiian Airlines ‘ohana who volunteered their time to help inspire and educate the next generation of future female leaders! 💜🌺

#PualaniProud #HawaiianAirlines #GIAD2024 #GirlsinAviation
